name: Chicken Tagine with Olives and Preserved Lemons
recipeIngredient: 2 3 lb chickens, each cut into 6 pieces
2 medium tomatoes, peeled, seeded and chopped
1 cup fresh flat-leaf parsley, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the olive oil in a large enameled cast-iron casserole.
2. Season the chicken pieces with salt and pepper, then add them to the casserole along with onion, ginger, cinnamon, and saffron.
3. Cook over high heat, turning the chicken occasionally, until browned all over (about 10 minutes).
4. Add tomatoes, garlic, parsley, cilantro, and 2 cups water; bring to a boil.
5. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer, stirring occasionally, until chicken is tender (about 1 hour). The recipe can be prepared up to this point up to 2 days ahead; cover and refrigerate. Rewarm before continuing.
6. Meanwhile, rinse preserved lemons under running water; pat dry. Quarter the lemons and discard pulp from one lemon. Finely chop peel; set aside for garnish.
7. Transfer chicken to a large plate. Strain cooking liquid back into casserole; boil until slightly thickened (about 10 minutes).
8. Add chopped preserved lemon and olives; simmer over moderate heat for 2 minutes.
9. Return chicken pieces to the dish, adding just enough sauce to coat each piece. Simmer until heated through.
10. Serve in a serving dish, pouring remaining sauce on top and garnishing with lemon quarters.
